name: claw-kit
description: Thin Codex adapter for claw-kit with fixed code-mode plan and Goal synchronization, per-plan turn reports, and SDK-owned truth and ADR closeout.

interface.shortDescription: Prompt-driven .claw harness with compact workflowGuidance and hook-collected plan reports.
interface.longDescription: Use Claw Kit through using-claw-kit as the single main-agent workflow. Continue recovered .claw state from returned workflowGuidance and use the complexity gate before creating new plans. On Codex, every plan mutation runs through the fixed code-mode driver, while canonical plan and session-binding transitions remain independent from hooks. Stop hooks collect one final assistant message per turn into the current plan or subplan report. A detached Codex SDK worker evaluates the completed plan and report together, deposits verified truth and ADR when warranted, and then requests recall indexing without burdening or blocking the main agent.
defaultPrompt: Start with using-claw-kit. If a .claw workflow is already recovered, follow its returned workflowGuidance before creating anything.

Start with using-claw-kit. When no task scope exists, score the request first; low-complexity work can skip the formal claw workflow, while higher-complexity work should create a plan and follow the returned workflowGuidance.

Start with using-claw-kit. Let seeded planning tasks invoke the configured planning skill only after formal claw workflow entry; truth and ADR deposition is owned by the Stop hook and SDK closeout worker.

Start with using-claw-kit. On Codex, run every claw plan mutation only through its fixed code-mode driver; the program consumes hostActions and the agent must not execute goalTool or split host calls.
